item.title: I tassi di interesse della BCE potrebbero salire a causa dell'aumento del prezzo del petrolio
item.description: L'aumento del prezzo del petrolio potrebbe portare a un aumento dei tassi di interesse della BCE.

item.title: La crisi energetica colpisce le economie asiatiche dipendenti dal Medio Oriente
item.description: La crisi energetica sta colpendo le economie asiatiche che dipendono fortemente dal Medio Oriente.

item.title: Global markets recoil as Rubio warns war in Iran could stretch for weeks
item.description: Comments from US secretary of state come as oil jumps to $114 and Wall Street stocks slide

item.title: La sicurezza economica è una sfida crescente per le economie mondiali
item.description: La sicurezza economica è una sfida crescente per le economie mondiali a causa della crescente instabilità geopolitica.

item.title: US Escalation Is the Most Likely Scenario in Iran
item.description: If Donald Trump walks away from the war with Iran now, the threat to shipping in the Strait of Hormuz will remain, risk premia on oil prices will stay permanently higher, and Trump’s own popularity will sink even further ahead of this year’s midterm elections. Despite the obvious risks, he has every reason to try to “finish the job.”

item.title: The future economic architecture of the eurozone
item.description: The Peterson Institute for International Economics is pleased to host a high-level panel discussion on the future economic architecture of the eurozone, featuring Spanish finance minister Carlos Cuerpo Caballero and ECB Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel, alongside leading experts Olivier Blanchard of PIIE and Ángel Ubide of Citadel. Following opening remarks by Cuerpo Caballero, the conversation explores how much monetary policy transmission and financial stability in the eurozone depend on deeper integration, the opportunities and challenges posed by rising long-term commitments to…

item.title: Energy crisis hits currencies of Asian economies reliant on Middle East
item.description: Energy crisis hits currencies of Asian economies reliant on Middle East

item.title: China baijiu boom town goes bust after Beijing's austerity order
item.description: China baijiu boom town goes bust after Beijing's austerity order

item.title: Karachi leverages Iran war, wins a year's worth of transshipment in 24 days
item.description: Karachi leverages Iran war, wins a year's worth of transshipment in 24 days

item.title: China chip sector targets 80% self-sufficiency with US in its sights
item.description: China chip sector targets 80% self-sufficiency with US in its sights

item.title: China's COSCO ships appear to abort Strait of Hormuz transit
item.description: China's COSCO ships appear to abort Strait of Hormuz transit

item.title: Air China logs sixth straight loss as rail competition bites and risks rise
item.description: Air China logs sixth straight loss as rail competition bites and risks rise

item.title: Drillers See Triple-Digit Crude and Hit the Brakes
item.description: Brent crude is trading over $100 per barrel, WTI has topped $90, but oil drillers in the world’s largest producer are cautious about their future plans. In fact, they are rather unhappy with the war in the Middle East, because it has made it harder to plan investments. On the face of it, everything is perfect, price-wise. WTI is trading much higher than what shale drillers need to be profitable. According to the latest Dallas Fed Energy Survey, the range of WTI profitable drilling price levels for the oil patch is between $62 per barrel for…

item.title: Why Spain Is Weathering Europe’s Energy Crunch Better Than Most
item.description: Europe has “sleepwalked into yet another energy crisis” watching energy prices spike across the continent for the second time in four years due to instability in global politics and energy trade. Four years after the Russian invasion of Ukraine sent global energy markets reeling, Europe has made significant progress weaning itself off of Russian oil and gas imports and building up its own energy supply chains. But the European Union still relies on imports for more than half of its energy needs, leaving it achingly vulnerable to the…

item.title: Why the Iran Conflict Is Good News for Chinese Automakers
item.description: A sharp rise in oil prices tied to the US-Israel confrontation with Iran is likely to speed up the global transition to electric vehicles, strengthening a shift that has already helped China overtake Japan as the world’s top car seller, according to South China Morning Post. Crude prices have surged past $100 a barrel amid fears of disruption to energy supplies, particularly through the Strait of Hormuz. US President Donald Trump escalated tensions by warning he would “obliterate” Iran’s power plants if shipping through…

item.title: Russia Warns of Force Majeure on Oil Cargoes After Port Disruptions
item.description: Russia’s oil exporters are warning buyers that cargoes from its key Baltic ports may not be delivered at all. Russian producers are now saying they may declare force majeure on cargoes from key Baltic Sea ports after a sustained wave of Ukrainian drone strikes knocked out critical infrastructure this week. The epicenter is Ust-Luga, one of Russia’s most important export terminals, where oil loadings have been halted since Wednesday following repeated strikes from Ukraine and a fire that, as of Friday, was still burning. Industry sources…

item.title: Bitcoin traders see 53% odds of sub-$66K BTC by April 24
item.description: Bitcoin price slumped on Friday as uncertainty over the US economy and war in Iran negatively impacted stock and crypto markets.

item.title: Middle East War Reignites the Energy Transition
item.description: The worst oil and gas supply shock in history has exposed the vulnerability of dependence on fossil fuel imports and is making renewables popular again. As governments scramble to contain the fallout from the energy shock, both in supply and prices, increased electrification in transportation and power generation is once again the talk of the town. As the war in the Middle East laid bare the shock of losing oil and gas supply, policymakers and analysts are once again considering the benefits of fossil fuel importers boosting the share of renewable…

item.title: Future US governments could crack down on crypto without clear rules: Coin Center
item.description: The CLARITY Act stalled in the Senate after banks, crypto firms, and lawmakers failed to reach an agreement on key provisions like allowing stablecoin yields.

item.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
item.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

item.title: Warren probes China-based Bitmain over US security concerns: Report
item.description: US authorities launched the “Operation Red Sunset” probe into Bitmain last year over potential espionage and grid risks.

item.title: Lummis says CLARITY Act will deliver 'strongest' developer protections
item.description: Crypto lawyer Jake Chervinsky said legislation covering crypto developer protections has been overshadowed by the intense focus on stablecoin yield in the CLARITY Act.
